content:
2 min read
Just now

--

As a B.Tech Computer Science student, I always wanted to gain practical experience by building real-world applications beyond academic coursework. My Web Development Internship at Oasis Infobyte provided me with exactly that opportunity.
Through the project-based learning approach offered by Oasis Infobyte, I was able to apply my knowledge of full-stack web development while exploring industry-standard technologies. The internship challenged me to solve practical problems, work with multiple technologies simultaneously, and understand how complete web applications are built from scratch.
You can learn more about the internship programs offered by Oasis Infobyte at https://oasisinfobyte.com.
Project Overview — PizzaPilot
For my internship project, I developed PizzaPilot — Pizza Delivery & Inventory Management Platform, a production-inspired full-stack MERN application that simulates a complete online pizza ordering and inventory management system.
The objective was to design a modern web application with separate User and Admin modules, secure authentication, online payment integration, real-time order tracking, and automated inventory management.
Key Features
User Module

• Secure JWT-based Authentication and Authorization

• User Registration with Email Verification

• Forgot Password functionality

• Interactive 4-Step Pizza Builder

• Choose Pizza Base

• Select Sauce

• Choose Cheese

• Select Vegetables

• Dynamic Price Calculation

• Order Summary before Checkout

• Razorpay Test Mode Payment Integration

• Real-Time Order Tracking using Socket.IO
Admin Module

• Separate Admin Login

• Inventory Management Dashboard

• Manual Stock Update

• Automatic Stock Deduction after Successful Orders

• Order Management Dashboard

• Real-Time Order Status Updates

• Dashboard Analytics and Statistics

• Automated Low-Stock Email Notifications using node-cron and Nodemailer
Technology Stack
Frontend

• React.js

• Vite

• Tailwind CSS

• Framer Motion

• React Hook Form
Backend

• Node.js

• Express.js
Database

• MongoDB Atlas

• Mongoose
Additional Technologies

• Razorpay (Test Mode)

• Socket.IO

• Nodemailer

• node-cron

• JWT Authentication

• Helmet

• CORS

• Rate Limiter
Challenges and Learning Experience
Throughout this project, I learned how different parts of a full-stack application work together. From designing REST APIs and managing MongoDB collections to implementing authentication, integrating Razorpay Test Mode, and building real-time communication using Socket.IO, every stage helped strengthen my development skills.
I also gained practical experience in backend security, environment configuration, payment verification, deployment, debugging, and project documentation. Solving deployment-related issues and integrating multiple technologies taught me the importance of patience, testing, and continuous learning.
Conclusion
My internship at Oasis Infobyte has been an excellent learning experience. It provided me with the opportunity to work on a comprehensive full-stack project that closely resembles real-world software development.
I am grateful to Oasis Infobyte for creating a platform where students can gain hands-on experience through practical projects. This internship has strengthened my confidence in MERN Stack development and motivated me to continue building impactful software solutions.
## Project Repository
🔗 GitHub Repository: https://github.com/varung-coder/OIBSIP
The repository contains the complete source code, project documentation, setup instructions, API endpoints, and screens
Thank you, Oasis Infobyte, for this wonderful learning opportunity!
